questions.question: Derive the work-energy theorem for a body under variable force along a straight path.
questions.answer: For variable force F(x) along x, infinitesimal work dW = F(x) dx. Total work W = ∫ F(x) dx. Using Newton's second law F = m dv/dt and dx = v dt → W = ∫ m v dv = 1/2 m v² – 1/2 m u² = ΔKE.

questions.question: A 2 kg block is pulled with force F = 10 + 5x N along x in meters from x=0 to x=2 m. Find the work done.
answer_outline: Set up integral of F dx

questions.question: Derive expression for potential energy stored in a spring when stretched or compressed from equilibrium.
questions.answer: Force F = kx. Infinitesimal work dW = F dx = kx dx. Total work W = ∫0^x kx dx = 1/2 k x² = PE stored.

questions.question: A body of mass 3 kg slides down a 10 m frictionless incline making 30° with horizontal. Find speed at bottom using energy method.
questions.answer: Vertical height h = 10 sin30° = 5 m. Using mgh = 1/2 mv² → 3×9.8×5 = 1/2×3×v² → v² = 98 → v ≈ 9.9 m/s

questions.question: Derive the expression for power in terms of force and velocity.
questions.answer: P = dW/dt = d(F × d)/dt = F × (dd/dt) = F × v (if F along velocity direction).

questions.question: A mass m is thrown vertically with speed u. Derive expressions for maximum height and potential energy at top.
questions.answer: At top, velocity v = 0, KE = 0, PE = mgh. Using KE_initial = PE_top → 1/2 mu² = mgh → h = u²/(2g), PE = mgh = 1/2 mu².

questions.question: Explain energy transformations in a roller coaster, including effects of friction and air resistance.
questions.answer: At top: PE max, KE low. Descending: PE → KE. Friction and air resistance convert some mechanical energy into heat, reducing total mechanical energy.

questions.question: A machine lifts 500 kg to 10 m in 5 s. Calculate power output and efficiency if input energy is 60 kJ.
Derive the relation between velocity and height for a body sliding on a frictionless curve using energy conservation.
questions.answer: PE_top + KE_top = PE_bottom + KE_bottom → mgh + 1/2 mv_top² = 1/2 mv_bottom² → v_bottom = √(v_top² + 2gh).

questions.question: Explain with example how non-conservative forces affect total mechanical energy and work done.
questions.answer: Friction/air resistance converts mechanical energy into heat, reducing KE + PE. Example: Sliding block slows down due to friction; extra work is needed to maintain motion.

questions.question: A 1 kg mass is attached to spring k = 100 N/m. Find speed when passing equilibrium if stretched by 0.2 m.
questions.answer: PE_spring = 1/2 k x² = 1/2×100×0.2² = 2 J. At equilibrium, KE = PE = 2 J → 1/2 m v² = 2 → v = 2 m/s

questions.question: A block moves under force F = 5x² N along x. Find work done from x=0 to x=2 m.
Explain how kinetic friction affects work done by a body moving on a horizontal surface.
questions.answer: Friction opposes motion, doing negative work, reducing KE. Extra work must be done by applied force to maintain constant speed.

questions.question: A projectile of mass m is fired vertically. Derive time to reach maximum height using energy considerations.
questions.answer: At max height, KE=0 → 1/2 m v² = mgh → h = v²/(2g). Time t = h/v_avg. For constant acceleration: t = v/g
answer_outline: Use KE = PE at max height
Use v_avg to find t or v/g

questions.question: Derive the expression for the efficiency of a machine in terms of energy lost due to friction.
questions.answer: Efficiency = (Useful work output / Total input energy) ×100 = [(Input energy – energy lost)/Input energy] ×100

questions.question: A block slides down two frictionless inclines from same height but different slopes. Compare final speeds.
questions.answer: Final speed depends only on vertical height due to energy conservation: v = √(2gh). Slope does not affect final speed.

questions.question: Explain how work done by non-conservative forces can be recovered by adding external energy.
questions.answer: Friction reduces mechanical energy; to maintain motion, external work (engine/motor) must supply lost energy to restore KE and PE.

questions.question: A block attached to a spring oscillates with amplitude 0.1 m. Calculate maximum KE and PE if mass = 0.5 kg, k = 50 N/m.
questions.answer: PE_max = 1/2 k x² = 1/2 ×50×0.1² = 0.25 J. KE_max = PE_max = 0.25 J

questions.question: Derive relation between velocity and height for pendulum using energy conservation.
questions.answer: At angle θ, height h = L(1 – cosθ). KE = total mechanical energy – PE = m g L (1 – cosθ) = 1/2 m v² → v = √[2 g L (1 – cosθ)]

questions.question: A block moves under combined forces: F1 = 10 N constant, F2 = 5x N along x. Find total work done from x=0 to x=3 m.
Explain energy losses in machines due to friction, air resistance, and deformation.
questions.answer: Friction converts mechanical energy to heat; air resistance dissipates energy; deformation absorbs energy, reducing useful work output.

questions.question: Derive formula for KE of a body moving along a curve under gravity using energy principles.
questions.answer: Total energy E = KE + PE = constant. KE = E_total – m g h → v = √(2/m × (E_total – m g h))
answer_outline: Express KE in terms of PE

questions.question: A particle moves under a variable force F(x) = 6x – x². Find work done from x=0 to x=3 m.
A body of mass m slides down a rough incline. Derive expression for work done against friction.
questions.answer: Friction f = μ m g cosθ, displacement along incline s. Work done W_friction = f × s = μ m g cosθ × s

questions.question: A body is acted upon by a force F = (3 + 2x) N along x. Find work done from x=0 to x=4 m.
